AR1.justification: The checklist and Go/No-go landed in the runbook-designated output file (tmp/release-readiness/2.4/checklist.md), and the agent gave explicit rationale for not touching decisions.md or the daybook since a specific runbook (not a session ritual) was invoked.

AR2.justification: Both procedural steps produced a concrete artifact on disk—the checklist file and the Go/No-go decision written into that same file—and the agent visibly stalled on the missing release-notes input by flagging it MISSING rather than fabricating.

AR3.justification: All path references in the response are workspace-relative (not absolute), and they resolve—including the correct flagging of the user-referenced tmp/release-2.4/notes.md as non-existent.

AR4.justification: The only write is a regenerable tmp/ scratch file and no append-only register was mutated, so re-running the runbook would overwrite the checklist cleanly without corrupting any durable history.
